title: Golden Globes 2026: Best Supporting Actor Prediction Scoring Analysis
text: Gold Derby provides detailed analysis of prediction scoring accuracy for the 2026 Golden Globes, including the Best Supporting Actor category. The data reveals that nearly 3,000 people participated in film predictions across 13 categories. Prediction experts and users showed varying levels of accuracy in forecasting the supporting actor race, which emerged as a moderately difficult category to predict. The competitive supporting actor field with multiple viable candidates from different films contributed to the challenge for predictors. Winners demonstrated the most accurate predictions through careful analysis of industry trends, past voting patterns, and current buzz around performances. The ceremony, hosted by Nikki Glaser, aired January 11 on CBS and Paramount+. Ultimately, Stellan Skarsgård's win for Sentimental Value validated those who predicted the established veteran actor would prevail in this competitive category. The prediction scoring analysis highlights the importance of understanding voting patterns, film momentum, and the specific preferences of Golden Globe voters when making awards predictions.
